posts.text: Canaccord Genuity: "The market currently suffers from Zoolander syndrome: it appears to have only “one look”: AI. We’ve thrown endless warning flags about the grid’s inability to sustain data center deployments. but the market couldn’t care less – for now. The AI trade rolls on. Non-stop. More cowbell. We, too, feel like we are taking crazy pills."
posts.author: zerohedge

posts.text: These products are usually classified as food rather than medicines, leaving manufacturers freer to make claims about benefits without the backing of rigorous trials https://www.economist.com/science-and-technology/2026/06/19/is-fortified-infant-formula-worth-it?taid=6a3708b7fbb36b00014b2093&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
Technology firms are reshaping the global investment landscape:

The IT sector now accounts for ~38% of the MSCI USA Index, which tracks large and mid-cap stocks of the US equity market, covering ~85% of the US market cap.

This percentage has surged +15 points over the last 4 years.

The IT sector also accounts for ~44% of the MSCI EM Index, which covers large and mid-cap stocks across 24 emerging market countries, representing ~85% of each country's market cap.

Since 2022, tech's contribution in this metric has more than doubled.

Over the same period, the IT sector's proportion of US investment-grade bond issuance has more than tripled, to a record ~20%.

Tech is all that matters right now.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
